entry.title: Re: Interested to host CodeJam

Hello Purusottam,

We are currently doing CodeJams on ABAP Cloud, SAP CAP and SAP Build. The plan is to extend on SAP Integration Suite may be next year.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam request (Bengaluru)
entry.content:
Hi Gaurav,

Currently, we do not yet have an expertise in AI topic for a CodeJam in Bangalore. We offer the below topics.

1. ABAP Cloud and RAP

2. Cloud Application Programming model (CAP)

3. SAP Build

Let me know if we can plan for the above topics.

entry.title: Re: Desktoberfest - this year as part of the Scavenger Hunt!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Note: We have reached our maximum registration capacity and RSVP for this event is now closed. For those who could not sign up this time, please wait for the next announcement in this region.

This CodeJam instance will be an ABAP CodeJam on the topic: SAP Business Technology Platform, ABAP Environment & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.  Join us for this 6 hour day of developer networking and hands-on coding exercises on the latest ABAP capabilities. 

Session Abstract:

This session introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, 1 December, 2023 in Bangalore, India from 09:00 - 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:

Bosch Global Software Technologies

No. 419 , 3rd floor Cyber Park  76-77 E-City Phs-1 Ban

Bengaluru(Electronic City) Karnataka 560 100

The instructors are Developer Advocates @sheenamk , Ajay Soreng and Shilpa Shankar

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you are willing to attend, register by clicking Yes button in the Will you be attending? section.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Combine SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to create full-stack ap
entry.content:
Hi Chaitanya,

Once you click on yes in the Will you be attending? section, that's basically you have registered for the event. We will notify you about the next activities when we are nearing the event date.

entry.title: Re: Wish to Host SAP Code Jam in Bengaluru
entry.content:
Hello Balaji,

It was good to meet you in person at SAP TechEd. Apologies for late reply as we were quite held up with TechEd activities. 

Thank you for showing interest to host a SAP Codejam.

Below few points will help you to clarify initial questions.

1. SAP Codejam is a SAP community event which means it's open to SAP community i.e customers/partners. 

2. We will create an event and open for registration. Anyone who registers should be allowed to attend the event.

3. SAP will not charge for this event. As a host, you need to provide us a training room set up to do the hands on exercises. We, the Developer Advocates will come to the venue where you want to host the Codejam and conduct it. 

4. The topic of ABAP Cloud and CAP is fine for us.

We can discuss over a meeting on the next steps.

Best Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: Want to host Code Jam at Walmart Bengaluru
entry.content:
Hello Kunal,

we can offer a CodeJam on ABAP Cloud and RAP. We do not yet have expertise on Integration Suite in Bangalore region. I will schedule a call to discuss further.

Best Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: SAP CodeJam on ABAP Cloud and RAP in Bangalore
entry.content:
Continuing the journey of SAP CodeJam in India, we concluded one CodeJam last week at Bosch Global Software Technologies, Bangalore. Topic was ABAP Cloud and RAP. Given Bangalore being the hub for developers, room was filled with 29 enthusiastic and energetic participants. Few had travelled from other cities as well.

We had a warm welcome by our host Sachin and the participants started to fill in the room quickly. The setup was good, a proper training room. All technical checks done and started at 9 AM dot with an introduction of who the Developer Advocates are and all the activities that we engage with the SAP Community.

 


All the participants tuned in


Quick introduction of the participants was a good way to know our audience better and it served as an ice breaker. This was followed by Overview of ABAP Cloud and ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (in short RAP)


Sheena giving an overview of ABAP Cloud


 


Ajay explaining RAP development flow


 

Hands-on exercises


The day flew by. We had a positive and collaborative experience during the hands-on exercise with the group. Everyone were actively involved in learning and supported each other during technical glitches like account set up, ADT issues...


Troubleshooting during the exercise



 


Time for a selfie while participants are busy with the exercises 😉


The entire group was quite actively engaged in discussing various uses cases and how to apply the new learning into their projects. We also had senior leadership team from Bosch drop by and boost the event.


 

All in all, this CodeJam event had a warm atmosphere with actively engaging participants. We had great questions which resulted in interesting discussions. It was time well spent with good networking and a smile on everyone's face made our day.

 


Networking lunch



Wrap up!


 

An overview of this CodeJam topic


It's noteworthy to give a brief overview of the topic now that you have a fair idea about how the CodeJam event went.

We started with a basic of exploring the different objects in ABAP Cloud. For all the ABAPers, it was interesting to know how to create ABAP objects without using SAP Gui.

We created a simple ABAP class fetching the data from a database table. Moved on to understand how to consume an external HTTP service by leveraging the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and view the results in a browser.

Exciting part was the overview of RAP model and applying it to the travel example. This included creation of database tables, CDS views, creation and enhancing the service and finally creating a fiori app

If you are interested in hosting a SAP CodeJam, all the details are here

entry.title: Re: We would like to Host Code Jam Session in General Mill -Powai
entry.content:
Hi Vaishali,

Mid Feb is doable. Let us know the topics at the earliest to plan better.

Best Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam on ABAP Cloud and RAP in Bangalore
entry.content:
Hello Sameera,

This is an in-person event only and no recordings available.

entry.title: Re: Fiori launchpad in SAP BTP in sap BTP trial account
entry.content: You cannot complete this tutorial in a BTP trial account. It's mentioned in the pre-requisites.

entry.title: International Day of Women and Girls in Science
entry.content:
Feb 11th marks the International Day of Women and Girls in Science. Have you ever wondered why we need to explicitly celebrate these events and encourage more women to strive in technology?

Firstly, this certainly raises awareness and highlights the importance of diversity in Science and Technology filed.

Secondly, such events provide a platform to address the challenges that women face globally and a common platform to share our experiences. These challenges can include gender bias, unequal opportunities, and workplace culture to be an inclusive environment.

Well, what better than a round table discussion involving Women Developer Advocates at SAP come together to share their insights and strategies for overcoming these challenges. It would allow participants to support and learn from one another, while also showcasing the diverse talents and perspectives of women in technology.

Watch out for the discussion by @noravonthenen @sheenamk @MichelleMoudy @Rekha_DR @Shilpa_Shankar moderated by @qmacro
